attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: {"trajectory": {"thought_0": "The question is asking about a specific historical event that led to the overthrow of King James II of England and its connection to the Boston Revolt. I will search for details about this revolution and its implications in the American colonies, particularly regarding the Boston Revolt.", "tool_name_0": "search_wikipedia", "tool_args_0": {"query": "Glorious Revolution and its impact on the Boston Revolt"}, "observation_0": ["Leisler's Rebellion | Leisler's Rebellion was an uprising in late 17th century colonial New York in which German American merchant and militia captain Jacob Leisler seized control of the colony's south and ruled it from 1689 to 1691. The uprising took place in the aftermath of Britain's Glorious Revolution and the 1689 Boston revolt in the Dominion of New England, which had included New York. The rebellion reflected colonial resentment against the policies of deposed King James II.", "1689 Boston revolt | The 1689 Boston revolt was a popular uprising on April 18, 1689 against the rule of Sir Edmund Andros, the governor of the Dominion of New England. A well-organized \"mob\" of provincial militia and citizens formed in the town of Boston—the capital of the dominion—and arrested dominion officials. Members of the Church of England were also taken into custody by the rebels if they were believed to sympathize with the administration of the dominion. Neither faction sustained casualties during the revolt. Leaders of the former Massachusetts Bay Colony then reclaimed control of the government. In other colonies, members of governments displaced by the dominion were returned to power.", "Rise to Rebellion | Rise to Rebellion is a 2001 historical fiction book by Jeff Shaara that tells the story of the events leading up to the American Revolution. The book spans from the Boston Massacre to the signing of the Declaration of Independence in 1776. The events of the American Revolution are portrayed through the perspectives of multiple characters, including Sentry Hugh White of the British army, John Adams, Benjamin Franklin, Lieutenant-General Thomas Gage, George Washington, Governor Thomas Hutchinson, Captain James Hall, Abigail Adams, Paul Revere, Dr. Joseph Warren, and Major John Pitcairn."], "thought_1": "I have gathered information about the Glorious Revolution and its connection to the Boston Revolt. The Glorious Revolution led to the overthrow of King James II, and this event sparked the 1689 Boston Revolt against Sir Edmund Andros, the governor of the Dominion of New England. The observation indicates that the Boston Revolt was a direct response to the changes in governance following the Glorious Revolution. I now have enough information to formulate an answer to the question.", "tool_name_1": "finish", "tool_args_1": {}, "observation_1": "Completed."}, "reasoning": "The Glorious Revolution of 1688 led to the overthrow of King James II of England, which significantly impacted the American colonies. Following this revolution, there was a shift in governance that prompted the 1689 Boston Revolt against Sir Edmund Andros, the governor of the Dominion of New England. The Boston Revolt was a direct response to the political changes initiated by the Glorious Revolution, as colonists sought to reclaim their local governance and resist the dominion's authority.", "answer": "The revolution that led to the overthrow of King James II of England was the Glorious Revolution, and its news prompted the Boston Revolt against Sir Edmund Andros in 1689."}

    instructions="Given the fields `question`, produce the fields `answer`.\n\nYou are an Agent. In each episode, you will be given the fields `question` as input.     next_tool_args = Field(annotation=dict[str, Any] required=True json_schema_extra={'__dspy_field_type': 'output', 'prefix': 'Next Tool Args:', 'desc': '${next_tool_args}'})

attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: ["Leisler's Rebellion | Leisler's Rebellion was an uprising in late 17th century colonial New York in which German American merchant and militia captain Jacob Leisler seized control of the colony's south and ruled it from 1689 to 1691. The uprising took place in the aftermath of Britain's Glorious Revolution and the 1689 Boston revolt in the Dominion of New England, which had included New York. The rebellion reflected colonial resentment against the policies of deposed King James II.", "1689 Boston revolt | The 1689 Boston revolt was a popular uprising on April 18, 1689 against the rule of Sir Edmund Andros, the governor of the Dominion of New England. A well-organized \"mob\" of provincial militia and citizens formed in the town of Boston—the capital of the dominion—and arrested dominion officials. Members of the Church of England were also taken into custody by the rebels if they were believed to sympathize with the administration of the dominion. Neither faction sustained casualties during the revolt. Leaders of the former Massachusetts Bay Colony then reclaimed control of the government. In other colonies, members of governments displaced by the dominion were returned to power.", "Rise to Rebellion | Rise to Rebellion is a 2001 historical fiction book by Jeff Shaara that tells the story of the events leading up to the American Revolution. The book spans from the Boston Massacre to the signing of the Declaration of Independence in 1776. The events of the American Revolution are portrayed through the perspectives of multiple characters, including Sentry Hugh White of the British army, John Adams, Benjamin Franklin, Lieutenant-General Thomas Gage, George Washington, Governor Thomas Hutchinson, Captain James Hall, Abigail Adams, Paul Revere, Dr. Joseph Warren, and Major John Pitcairn."]

attributes.mlflow.spanInputs:
{"question": "what revolution led to the overthrow of King James II of England and its news led to overthrow of androws in the Boston Revolt", "trajectory": "[[ ## thought_0 ## ]]
The question is asking about a specific historical event that led to the overthrow of King James II of England and its connection to the Boston Revolt. This suggests that I need to gather information about the Glorious Revolution, which is known for leading to James II's overthrow. I will search for details about this revolution and its implications in the American colonies, particularly regarding the Boston Revolt.

[[ ## tool_name_0 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_0 ## ]]
{"query": "Glorious Revolution and its impact on the Boston Revolt"}

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «Leisler's Rebellion | Leisler's Rebellion was an uprising in late 17th century colonial New York in which German American merchant and militia captain Jacob Leisler seized control of the colony's south and ruled it from 1689 to 1691. The uprising took place in the aftermath of Britain's Glorious Revolution and the 1689 Boston revolt in the Dominion of New England, which had included New York. The rebellion reflected colonial resentment against the policies of deposed King James II.»
[2] «1689 Boston revolt | The 1689 Boston revolt was a popular uprising on April 18, 1689 against the rule of Sir Edmund Andros, the governor of the Dominion of New England. A well-organized "mob" of provincial militia and citizens formed in the town of Boston—the capital of the dominion—and arrested dominion officials. Members of the Church of England were also taken into custody by the rebels if they were believed to sympathize with the administration of the dominion. Neither faction sustained casualties during the revolt. Leaders of the former Massachusetts Bay Colony then reclaimed control of the government. In other colonies, members of governments displaced by the dominion were returned to power.»
[3] «Rise to Rebellion | Rise to Rebellion is a 2001 historical fiction book by Jeff Shaara that tells the story of the events leading up to the American Revolution. The book spans from the Boston Massacre to the signing of the Declaration of Independence in 1776. The events of the American Revolution are portrayed through the perspectives of multiple characters, including Sentry Hugh White of the British army, John Adams, Benjamin Franklin, Lieutenant-General Thomas Gage, George Washington, Governor Thomas Hutchinson, Captain James Hall, Abigail Adams, Paul Revere, Dr. Joseph Warren, and Major John Pitcairn.»"}
attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: {"next_thought": "I have gathered information about the Glorious Revolution and its connection to the Boston Revolt. The Glorious Revolution led to the overthrow of King James II, and this event sparked the 1689 Boston Revolt against Sir Edmund Andros, the governor of the Dominion of New England. The observation indicates that the Boston Revolt was a direct response to the changes in governance following the Glorious Revolution. I now have enough information to formulate an answer to the question.", "next_tool_name": "finish", "next_tool_args": {}}

The uprising took place in the aftermath of Britain's Glorious Revolution and the 1689 Boston revolt in the Dominion of New England, which had included New York. The rebellion reflected colonial resentment against the policies of deposed King James II.»
[2] «1689 Boston revolt | The 1689 Boston revolt was a popular uprising on April 18, 1689 against the rule of Sir Edmund Andros, the governor of the Dominion of New England. A well-organized "mob" of provincial militia and citizens formed in the town of Boston—the capital of the dominion—and arrested dominion officials. Members of the Church of England were also taken into custody by the rebels if they were believed to sympathize with the administration of the dominion. Neither faction sustained casualties during the revolt. Leaders of the former Massachusetts Bay Colony then reclaimed control of the government. In other colonies, members of governments displaced by the dominion were returned to power.»
[3] «Rise to Rebellion | Rise to Rebellion is a 2001 historical fiction book by Jeff Shaara that tells the story of the events leading up to the American Revolution. The book spans from the Boston Massacre to the signing of the Declaration of Independence in 1776. The events of the American Revolution are portrayed through the perspectives of multiple characters, including Sentry Hugh White of the British army, John Adams, Benjamin Franklin, Lieutenant-General Thomas Gage, George Washington, Governor Thomas Hutchinson, Captain James Hall, Abigail Adams, Paul Revere, Dr. Joseph Warren, and Major John Pitcairn.»
